14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: This Part 43 appendix prescribes the test procedures and tolerances used when inspecting aircraft altimeters.
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: Appendix E to Part 43 is the authoritative source for altimeter test procedures and tolerances.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: A shop needs this type of FAA certificate, with an appropriate rating, to legally perform pitot-static certification tests.
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: Part 145 is the repair station rule.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: These Parts 23 and 25 sections establish the maximum allowable leak rate for aircraft pitot-static systems.
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: 14 CFR 23.1325 (and 25.1325 for transport) sets the leak rate; 91.411 and 91.413 are operating-rule check intervals.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: For a pressurized airframe, the static-system leak allowance is stated as either 2% of cabin differential or this value, taking whichever number is larger.
choices: Zero leak with no tolerance permitted
50 feet per minute for five minutes
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: 2% of test pressure or 100 feet, whichever is greater, is the limit.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: Technicians set the test chamber to this indicated altitude, referenced to mean sea level, when running the altimeter case-leak check.
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: Case leak check is at 18,000 ft MSL.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: During an altimeter case-leak evaluation, this figure represents the greatest pointer drift tolerated over a one-minute window.
choices: Zero leak permitted at any altitude
Plus or minus 100 feet per minute
A positive 100 feet per minute only
Plus or minus 10 feet per minute
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: Case leak drift tolerance is 100 fpm either direction.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: This is the authoritative source for the test procedures and tolerances used during an RVSM inspection.
choices: OEM or STC holder data package
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: The OEM or STC holder RVSM data package is the source.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: Only this standard's tolerances apply to the standby altimeter during an RVSM inspection.
choices: The same RVSM tolerances as the primary
No testing is required on the standby
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: Standby altimeters are tested to 91.411 tolerances.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: Aircraft flying inside this band of flight levels are subject to RVSM rules and 1,000-foot vertical spacing.
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: RVSM airspace spans FL290 through FL410.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: Within reduced vertical separation airspace, this number represents the largest combined altimetry error the aircraft may exhibit.
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: Total vertical error limit is +/-245 feet.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: This FAA-issued document specifically authorizes an operator to fly an equipped and tested aircraft in RVSM airspace.
choices: None if the equipment passes testing
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: An aircraft-specific RVSM LOA is required.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: This is required after an RVSM altitude deviation before the aircraft may return to RVSM operations.
choices: A logbook entry of the pilot report only
Replacement of every altimeter on the aircraft
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: System inspection per approved RVSM data is required.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: This determines the recurring RVSM certification interval, which is typically 12 or 24 months.
choices: OEM or STC holder data, typically 12 or 24 months
14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.explanation: OEM or STC holder data sets the interval.

14 CFR 65.91 Regulations.clue: Compared with the baseline requirements of Part 91.411, the altimeter accuracy limits imposed for RVSM operation are described this way.
